codeReview.summary: The tools-git-ops feature successfully implements GitTool with git_status, git_diff, git_log, git_commit operations and create_branch with agent/<task-id>/<description> naming convention. The implementation in crates/swell-tools/src/tools.rs provides structured git operations with proper error handling. However, the commit ID 491e98c only contains a minor fix (removing unused mut), not the actual GitTool implementation - the GitTool was already present from earlier commits (d5d9ae0).

issues.description: GitTool's git_status parses porcelain output manually. The current implementation may not handle all git status formats correctly (e.g., 'initial branch' messages, 'future parent' syntax). Consider using git2 or a more robust parsing approach for production use.

git_diff only uses --stat flag by default. The feature description mentions 'git_diff' without specifying the output format. Users expecting full diff output may be surprised. Consider adding an option for full diff output.

git_log uses --oneline --decorate which is good, but the feature description doesn't specify the log format. The current implementation limits to --oneline which may not capture all needed information.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Rust skill (SKILL.md) provides general Rust guidance but does not document project-specific patterns. The worker followed the skill's general guidance but the project has specific patterns (e.g., using async_trait, tracing for logging, thiserror for errors) that aren't in the skill.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker read mission.md and AGENTS.md for project conventions. The skill file is general Rust guidance without project-specific conventions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md defines project structure and coding conventions, but the branch naming convention 'agent/<task-id>/<description>' mentioned in the feature description is not documented in AGENTS.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature description states 'Branch naming: agent/<task-id>/<description>' but AGENTS.md only contains general git commands without branch naming conventions.

summary: The tools-git-ops feature PASSES review. GitTool is fully implemented with git_status, git_diff, git_log, git_commit operations and create_branch with the agent/<task-id>/<description> naming convention. All git-related tests pass (10 tests). The commit ID 491e98c is a minor fix; the actual GitTool implementation was part of earlier commits. No blocking issues found. Minor non-blocking observations: git status parsing could be more robust, and branch naming convention is not documented in AGENTS.md.
